meaning: n.[贬](穿号衣的)仆人,奴才,走狗,小人
examples: She is a Hillary flunky who lost big.
This is psychology of a kind of flunky.
When I say this, I feel me like a snobbish flunky.
He buzzes for a flunky, who spares his boss the indignity of picking up the pink ring binders piled on the floor.
The star, who is seriously ill and beginning a bitter withdrawal from life, takes one of the young men (Rogen) into his house as a gag writer, punching bag, flunky, and nurse.
